sentences.quote: We present a unified, machine-verified treatment of four anomaly constraints on Standard-Model particle content.
sentences.quote_normalized: we present a unified, machine-verified treatment of four anomaly constraints on standard-model particle content.
sentences.agent_notes: Meta-claim about the paper's scope.

meta.quote_normalized: we present a unified, machine-verified treatment of four anomaly constraints on standard-model particle content.

sentences.quote: The chiral central charge $c_{-} = 8 N_f$ derived from sixteen left-handed Weyl components per generation, combined with the modular framing constraint $24 \mid c_{-}$ from $\eta(\tau{+}1) = e^{2\pi i/24}\eta(\tau)$, forces $N_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the chiral central charge $c_{-} = 8 n_f$ derived from sixteen left-handed weyl components per generation, combined with the modular framing constraint $24 \mid c_{-}$ from $\eta(\tau{+}1) = e^{2\pi i/24}\eta(\tau)$, forces $n_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$.
sentences.agent_notes: Three theorems chain to deliver N_f ≡ 0 (mod 3); all FOUND in lean_deps.json. Wang2024 cite resolved (arxiv:2312.14928).

meta.quote_normalized: the chiral central charge $c_{-} = 8 n_f$ derived from sixteen left-handed weyl components per generation, combined with the modular framing constraint $24 \mid c_{-}$ from $\eta(\tau{+}1) = e^{2\pi i/24}\eta(\tau)$, forces $n_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$.

sentences.quote: The minimal solution $N_f = 3$ is verified through Mathlib's \texttt{Complex.exp\_add}; without right-handed neutrinos $c_{-} = 15/2$ is fractional, giving an independent gravitational-anomaly argument for $\nu_R$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the minimal solution $n_f = 3$ is verified through mathlib's complex.exp\_add; without right-handed neutrinos $c_{-} = 15/2$ is fractional, giving an independent gravitational-anomaly argument for $\nu_r$.
sentences.agent_notes: Both target theorems FOUND in lean_deps.json. Independent ν_R argument is consistent with the Lean module structure.

meta.quote_normalized: the minimal solution $n_f = 3$ is verified through mathlib's complex.exp\_add; without right-handed neutrinos $c_{-} = 15/2$ is fractional, giving an independent gravitational-anomaly argument for $\nu_r$.

sentences.quote: The algebraic core uses $\dim_{\mathbb{F}_2} \mathrm{Ext}^n_{A(1)}(\mathbb{F}_2,\mathbb{F}_2) = 1,2,2,2,3,4$ for $n = 0,\dots,5$ together with the change-of-rings adjunction $\mathrm{Hom}_A(A \otimes_{A(1)} M, N) \cong \mathrm{Hom}_{A(1)}(M, N)$ --- the first machine-checked Ext computation over a Steenrod subalgebra.
sentences.quote_normalized: the algebraic core uses $\dim_{\mathbb{f}_2} ext^n_{a(1)}(\mathbb{f}_2,\mathbb{f}_2) = 1,2,2,2,3,4$ for $n = 0,\dots,5$ together with the change-of-rings adjunction $hom_a(a \otimes_{a(1)} m, n) \cong hom_{a(1)}(m, n)$ --- the first machine-checked ext computation over a steenrod subalgebra.
sentences.agent_notes: A1Ext module exists (14 decls). The specific name 'hom_tensor_adjunction' is MISSING; closest match is 'hom_tensor_adjunction_dim' in ChangeOfRings module (paper §2.6 references the missing name explicitly — see TN finding there). Ext dimensions 1,2,2,2,3,4 reflect literature canonical values.

meta.quote_normalized: the algebraic core uses $\dim_{\mathbb{f}_2} ext^n_{a(1)}(\mathbb{f}_2,\mathbb{f}_2) = 1,2,2,2,3,4$ for $n = 0,\dots,5$ together with the change-of-rings adjunction $hom_a(a \otimes_{a(1)} m, n) \cong hom_{a(1)}(m, n)$ --- the first machine-checked ext computation over a steenrod subalgebra.

sentences.quote: The Z$_4$ generator $X = 5(B-L) - 4Y$ assigns odd Z$_4$ charge to all six SM fermion species; $3 \times 15 \equiv -3 \pmod{16}$ in the no-$\nu_R$ case forces a hidden Z$_{16}$-charged sector.
sentences.quote_normalized: the z$_4$ generator $x = 5(b-l) - 4y$ assigns odd z$_4$ charge to all six sm fermion species; $3 \times 15 \equiv -3 \pmod{16}$ in the no-$\nu_r$ case forces a hidden z$_{16}$-charged sector.
sentences.agent_notes: Theorem hidden_sector_required FOUND. Z_4 generator and 6 SM species residues 6+3+3+2+1+1=16 are arithmetically correct. Numerical 3×15=45≡-3 (mod 16) verified.

meta.quote_normalized: the z$_4$ generator $x = 5(b-l) - 4y$ assigns odd z$_4$ charge to all six sm fermion species; $3 \times 15 \equiv -3 \pmod{16}$ in the no-$\nu_r$ case forces a hidden z$_{16}$-charged sector.

sentences.quote: A first-of-kind formal synthesis of the chirality wall integrates a no-go pillar (Golterman-Shamir nine conditions, two-particle-fermion (TPF) evasion via discontinuity of \texttt{round} at 1/2 and infinite-dimensionality of $\ell^2(\mathbb{Z})$), a positive-construction pillar (Gioia-Thorngren BdG Hamiltonian on $(\mathbb{Z}/L)^3$ with the central commutation $[H_\text{BdG}(p),\, q_A(p)] = 0$ closed by an Aristotle-discovered $\sin^2 + \cos^2 = 1$ identity), and an algebraic pillar (Onsager Dolan-Grady, Inönü-Wigner contraction $[\varepsilon A_m, \varepsilon A_n] = 4\varepsilon^2 G_{m-n}$, $\mathrm{su}(2)$ emanant symmetry, Witten anomaly $\pi_4(\mathrm{SU}(2)) = \mathbb{Z}_2 \hookrightarrow \mathbb{Z}_{16}$).
sentences.quote_normalized: a first-of-kind formal synthesis of the chirality wall integrates a no-go pillar (golterman-shamir nine conditions, two-particle-fermion (tpf) evasion via discontinuity of round at 1/2 and infinite-dimensionality of $\ell^2(\mathbb{z})$), a positive-construction pillar (gioia-thorngren bdg hamiltonian on $(\mathbb{z}/l)^3$ with the central commutation $[h_bdg(p),\, q_a(p)] = 0$ closed by an aristotle-discovered $\sin^2 + \cos^2 = 1$ identity), and an algebraic pillar (onsager dolan-grady, inönü-wigner contraction $[\varepsilon a_m, \varepsilon a_n] = 4\varepsilon^2 g_{m-n}$, $su(2)$ emanant symmetry, witten anomaly $\pi_4(su(2)) = \mathbb{z}_2 \hookrightarrow \mathbb{z}_{16}$).
sentences.agent_notes: All three pillars have backing modules. GTCommutation.gt_commutation_4x4 FOUND. ChiralityWallMaster exists with 35 decls.

meta.quote_normalized: a first-of-kind formal synthesis of the chirality wall integrates a no-go pillar (golterman-shamir nine conditions, two-particle-fermion (tpf) evasion via discontinuity of round at 1/2 and infinite-dimensionality of $\ell^2(\mathbb{z})$), a positive-construction pillar (gioia-thorngren bdg hamiltonian on $(\mathbb{z}/l)^3$ with the central commutation $[h_bdg(p),\, q_a(p)] = 0$ closed by an aristotle-discovered $\sin^2 + \cos^2 = 1$ identity), and an algebraic pillar (onsager dolan-grady, inönü-wigner contraction $[\varepsilon a_m, \varepsilon a_n] = 4\varepsilon^2 g_{m-n}$, $su(2)$ emanant symmetry, witten anomaly $\pi_4(su(2)) = \mathbb{z}_2 \hookrightarrow \mathbb{z}_{16}$).

sentences.quote: The integer 16 appears in four formally distinct settings (SM Weyl count, Z$_{16}$ DIII bordism index, Rokhlin's signature theorem, Kitaev DIII period-16) and the generation count $N_f = 3$ appears in two independent settings (Z$_{16}$ + modular).
sentences.quote_normalized: the integer 16 appears in four formally distinct settings (sm weyl count, z$_{16}$ diii bordism index, rokhlin's signature theorem, kitaev diii period-16) and the generation count $n_f = 3$ appears in two independent settings (z$_{16}$ + modular).
sentences.agent_notes: IA-class P3 anti-pattern check: the 4 settings are semantically distinct (fermion content, cobordism group, 4-manifold signature, free-fermion phase periodic table). Not the same setting restated. PASS.

meta.quote_normalized: the integer 16 appears in four formally distinct settings (sm weyl count, z$_{16}$ diii bordism index, rokhlin's signature theorem, kitaev diii period-16) and the generation count $n_f = 3$ appears in two independent settings (z$_{16}$ + modular).

sentences.quote: The end-to-end chain --- from sixteen Weyl components through three formal mechanisms to one observed $N_f = 3$ --- is verified by \texttt{lake build} on a 5229-theorem, 243-module Lean 4 library with one axiom and zero \texttt{sorry}; 322 of those theorems were closed by the Aristotle automated prover.
sentences.quote_normalized: the end-to-end chain --- from sixteen weyl components through three formal mechanisms to one observed $n_f = 3$ --- is verified by lake build on a 5229-theorem, 243-module lean 4 library with one axiom and zero sorry; 322 of those theorems were closed by the aristotle automated prover.
sentences.agent_notes: Verified against lean_deps.json: 5229 theorems, 243 modules, 1 project axiom (gapped_interface_axiom), and ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS in constants.py = 322. Zero sorry verified by grep on actual .lean files (4 lone-sorry matches all in `/-` docstring/comment blocks; library builds clean).

sentences.quote: The single axiom (\texttt{gapped\_interface\_axiom}, a 4+1D hypothesis) is corroborated in 1+1D and 2+1D by independent formalizations.
sentences.quote_normalized: the single axiom (gapped\_interface\_axiom, a 4+1d hypothesis) is corroborated in 1+1d and 2+1d by independent formalizations.

sentences.quote: Three textbook topology results (ko cohomology, Adams spectral sequence convergence, Anderson-Brown-Peterson splitting) enter as tracked hypotheses pending Mathlib algebraic-topology infrastructure.

However HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY contains 5 active tracked hypotheses (rokhlin_sigma_mod_16, modular_invariance_framing, c_minus_equals_8Nf, characteristic_square_mod_8) that the paper does NOT explicitly name-disclose, even though it invokes their dependent theorems (sixteen_convergence_full, generation_constraint_iff, central_charge_from_sm). The 'three textbook hypotheses' claim is technically accurate but undercounts the disclosable surface.

sentences.quote: The Standard Model has three generations of chiral fermions. No mechanism in the original SM Lagrangian explains why three rather than two or twelve.

sentences.quote: This paper performs the first machine-verified unification of the four threads.
sentences.quote_normalized: this paper performs the first machine-verified unification of the four threads.
sentences.agent_notes: First-claim assertion. Should be spot-checked via qi-firstclaimverification per bundle prompts §D2.

sentences.quote: What is new here is the integrated treatment that exposes the load-bearing axiom budget when all four threads are placed inside a single Lean 4 library: one true axiom (\texttt{gapped\_interface\_axiom}, the 4+1D conjecture sitting at the intersection of mechanisms (ii) and (iii), supported by machine-checked 1+1D and 2+1D evidence), three tracked algebraic-topology hypotheses (deferred to upstream Mathlib), and a single Mathlib-deferral placeholder (\texttt{dai\_freed\_spin\_z4}).
sentences.quote_normalized: what is new here is the integrated treatment that exposes the load-bearing axiom budget when all four threads are placed inside a single lean 4 library: one true axiom (gapped\_interface\_axiom, the 4+1d conjecture sitting at the intersection of mechanisms (ii) and (iii), supported by machine-checked 1+1d and 2+1d evidence), three tracked algebraic-topology hypotheses (deferred to upstream mathlib), and a single mathlib-deferral placeholder (dai\_freed\_spin\_z4).
sentences.agent_notes: TN-class drift on dai_freed_spin_z4: registered in module SKEFTHawking.Z16AnomalyComputation, NOT in Z16Classification (where paper §3.3 places it via 'Z16Classification.dai_freed_spin_z4'). The bare name 'dai_freed_spin_z4' does exist as a theorem (kind='theorem' in lean_deps), but the paper's later reference to Z16Classification.dai_freed_spin_z4 (§3.3 line 413) is the one that fails the Class TN module-name resolution.

meta.quote_normalized: what is new here is the integrated treatment that exposes the load-bearing axiom budget when all four threads are placed inside a single lean 4 library: one true axiom (gapped\_interface\_axiom, the 4+1d conjecture sitting at the intersection of mechanisms (ii) and (iii), supported by machine-checked 1+1d and 2+1d evidence), three tracked algebraic-topology hypotheses (deferred to upstream mathlib), and a single mathlib-deferral placeholder (dai\_freed\_spin\_z4).

sentences.quote: Falsification at this level would be either the discovery of an internally consistent SM extension violating $N_f \equiv 0 \pmod 3$ (which would refute the modular hypothesis of~\cite{Wang2024} as currently stated) or the exhibition of a 4+1D construction with a gapped boundary violating the SPT-stacking trivialisation rule (which would refute \texttt{gapped\_interface\_axiom}).
sentences.quote_normalized: falsification at this level would be either the discovery of an internally consistent sm extension violating $n_f \equiv 0 \pmod 3$ (which would refute the modular hypothesis of~ as currently stated) or the exhibition of a 4+1d construction with a gapped boundary violating the spt-stacking trivialisation rule (which would refute gapped\_interface\_axiom).
sentences.agent_notes: Falsifiability section: identifies two concrete falsifiers tied to the modular hypothesis and the sole live axiom. Sound.

sentences.quote: The Lean formalisation~\cite{Roehm2026Wave10} encodes the divisibility through the modular $q$-parameter shift in \texttt{ModularInvarianceConstraint.qParam\_shift}, which uses Mathlib's \texttt{Complex.exp\_add} to establish $q^{(\tau+1)} = q^{(\tau)} \cdot e^{2\pi i/24}$ on the $\eta$-function side, and reads back $24 \mid c_{-}$ in \texttt{ModularInvarianceConstraint.framing\_anomaly\_constraint}.

sentences.agent_notes: Both theorem names FOUND. Citation Roehm2026Wave10 is MISSING from CITATION_REGISTRY (in-prep self-cite; CitationIntegrity flag — Class CI). Other in-prep self-cites Roehm2026Wave1/4/5/6f/6g/Pipeline are in registry, so this is an unregistered key gap.

sentences.quote: On the Rokhlin side, the signature of a closed spin 4-manifold is divisible by 16~\cite{Rokhlin1952}; the algebraic-Rokhlin Lean module establishes the corresponding $8 \mid \sigma$ refinement via the unimodular intersection form of $E_8$, with \texttt{E8Lattice.sigma\_E8\_eq\_8} discharged by \texttt{native\_decide}.

sentences.agent_notes: TN-class: theorem name 'E8Lattice.sigma_E8_eq_8' is MISSING from lean_deps.json. The E8Lattice module exists (19 decls; e.g. e8_det_is_unit, e8_det_one) but no decl with name 'sigma_E8_eq_8' or any name containing 'sigma_E8'. Possible drift after a Lean refactor; the symbol or its module home appears to have been renamed/removed.

meta.quote_normalized: on the rokhlin side, the signature of a closed spin 4-manifold is divisible by 16~; the algebraic-rokhlin lean module establishes the corresponding $8 \mid \sigma$ refinement via the unimodular intersection form of $e_8$, with e8lattice.sigma\_e8\_eq\_8 discharged by native\_decide.

sentences.quote: The full chain is encoded in \texttt{RokhlinBridge.sixteen\_convergence\_full}. Crucially, the combination of $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ + modular constraints with no $\nu_R$ forces $N_f \in {\rm lcm}(16, 3) \cdot \mathbb{Z} = 48 \mathbb{Z}$, a result encoded in \texttt{RokhlinBridge.constraints\_without\_nu\_R}: the observed $N_f = 3$ is therefore not merely consistent with the modular constraint; under no-$\nu_R$ it is incompatible with it. The joint argument is strictly stronger than either piece in isolation, as established in \texttt{RokhlinBridge.rokhlin\_strictly\_stronger}.

sentences.quote: A direct check on each of the six SM fermion species with the $\nu_R$-completed convention gives $X[Q_L] = X[u_R] = X[d_R] = X[L_L] = X[e_R] = X[\nu_R] \equiv 1 \pmod 4$, i.e., every Weyl component carries odd Z$_4$ charge. This is encoded exhaustively in \texttt{SMFermionData} (48 theorems total).

sentences.quote: Per generation, the anomaly contribution is the sum of the squared Z$_4$ charges weighted by the multiplicity in the generator basis: $\sum_f n_f X_f^2 \equiv 6 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 1 + 1 = 16 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$.

sentences.quote: Composing across $N_f = 3$ generations without $\nu_R$ gives $3 \times 15 = 45 \equiv -3 \pmod{16}$. A single chiral hidden sector with Z$_4$ charge totalling $+3 \bmod 16$ would saturate the anomaly. This is the formal statement at the heart of \texttt{Z16AnomalyComputation.hidden\_sector\_required}: any internally consistent SM extension that omits $\nu_R$ requires either (a) BSM Z$_{16}$-charged matter or (b) a non-trivial gravitational-anomaly cancellation route. The proof contains 23 substantive theorems, no axioms, no \texttt{sorry}.

sentences.quote: This paper makes three falsifiable predictions: (i) any internally consistent SM extension violating $N_f \equiv 0 \pmod 3$ falsifies Wang's modular hypothesis as currently stated; (ii) any 4+1D construction admitting a gapped boundary violating SPT-stacking trivialisation falsifies the project's sole axiom; (iii) any lattice chiral fermion construction with chirality-charge spectrum bounded inside $\{-1, +1\}$ on finite-dimensional Hilbert space falsifies the chirality-wall pillar-1$\leftrightarrow$pillar-2 bridge.

sentences.agent_notes: Class CI WARN: paper bibitem says 'arXiv:1008.4138' but CITATION_REGISTRY['FidkowskiKitaev2010'].arxiv = '0904.2197'. arXiv-ID drift between paper text and registry. 1008.4138 → arXiv 'Topological phases of fermions in one dimension' Fidkowski-Kitaev (2011 PRB 83, 075103); 0904.2197 → 'Effects of interactions on the topological classification of free fermion systems' (2010 PRB 81, 134509). These are TWO DIFFERENT FK PAPERS. Paper bibitem cites the 2011 1D-classification paper (matches the cited 'PRB 83, 075103' coords); registry stub has the 2010 interaction-reduction paper. Either rebind registry to 1008.4138 or add a second entry.
